Figure 1 . Heracles and Geryon. Depiction on a Chalcidian amphora produced in Calabria (c. 530 BCE ), Cabinet des Médailles, Paris (De Ridder.202) with painted inscription: ATHENAIE, GARUWONES, HERAKLES. Photo: Serge Oboukhoff / BnF-CNRS-MSH Mondes © License: CC BY-NC.
Figure 2 . Depiction on the golden bowl of Hasanlu (detail), north-west Iran (late 2nd millennium BCE ). Image 96557, courtesy of the Penn Museum © License: CC BY-NC.

Figure 3 . Snakes and tricephalic monster with an axe and a tethered horned animal. Eighteenth-century rendering of a depiction of the fifth-century CE lesser horn (B) from Gallehus. Photo: Malene Thyssen. From: Wikimedia Commons. License: CC-PD.
Figure 1 . The Churning of the Ocean. From: Wikimedia Commons. License: CC-PD.
Figure 2 . Churning butter. From: WebExhibits. License: CC BY-NC.
Figure 1 . Fertile fields covered by snow. Håga Valley in Uppsala, Sweden, 6 January 2022. Photo: Terje Oestigaard © License: CC BY-NC.
Figure 2 . Frey and wild-boar. By Jacques Reich (1852–1923). From: Guerber 1909: 118. License: CC-PD.
Figure 3 . The sacrifice of Domalde in Old Uppsala. By Erik Werenskiold (1855–1938). From: Snorri 2011 [1899]. License: CC-PD.
Figure 4 . Terrestrial and celestial forces at work in agrarian cosmologies. Graphics: Terje Oestigaard © License: CC BY-NC.
Figure 5 . Growth forces living and manifesting themselves beneath the snow in early spring. Photo: Terje Oestigaard © License: CC BY-NC.
Figure 6 . The never-freezing Ingbokällarna, Sweden. Photo: Terje Oestigaard © License: CC BY-NC.
Figure 7 . The Håga mound covered in snow, 6 January 2022. Photo: Terje Oestigaard © License: CC BY-NC.
Figure 8 . Fertile fields in the Håga Valley, Uppsala. Photo: Terje Oestigaard © License: CC BY-NC.

corrections_applied.ligature_repairs: not applied beyond source XHTML character decoding
corrections_applied.laryngeal_normalization: applied to class-based subscript spans where detected; otherwise source characters preserved
corrections_applied.paragraph_rejoins: not applicable to EPUB text layer; source paragraph structure preserved
